title: NIA Court Sentences Kashmiri Separatist Asiya Andrabi to Life Imprisonment
detail: A Delhi National Investigation Agency (NIA) court sentenced prominent Kashmiri separatist leader Asiya Andrabi — head of Dukhtaran-e-Millat — to life imprisonment under the Unlawful Activities (Prevention) Act (UAPA) for funding terrorism and sedition. Her associates Fehmeeda Sofi and Nahida Nasreen received 30-year sentences each. Pakistan's Foreign Office strongly condemned the verdict as 'politically driven,' while Mirwaiz Umar Farooq and Mehbooba Mufti called for compassionate review given Andrabi's age and health. APHC-AJK wrote to the UN Secretary-General calling the conviction fabricated.

media.caption: NIA court sentences Kashmiri separatist Asiya Andrabi to life imprisonment

title: Pakistan Chargé d'Affaires: 'Kashmir Solution Key to Durable Peace'
detail: Pakistan's Chargé d'Affaires Saad Ahmad Warraich, speaking at a Pakistan Day ceremony at the Pakistan High Commission in New Delhi, stated that durable peace in South Asia is impossible without a just resolution of the Kashmir dispute in line with UN resolutions and the aspirations of the Kashmiri people. The event marked one of the few continuing diplomatic interactions between India and Pakistan following the near-total downgrade of relations after Operation Sindoor in May 2025.

media.caption: Pakistan envoy says Kashmir solution is key to durable peace
